summary: Broad HN scout pass highlighting agentic coding, local model tooling, and a few retro/education gems.

items.name: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B
items.source_story_title: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B: Agentic coding power, now open to all
items.summary: Open-weight 35B model aimed at agentic coding, with commenters comparing it favorably to Opus 4.7.
items.why_included: It drew strong local-run and benchmark chatter, making it the clearest open-model pickup of the day.
items.evidence: A commenter said they were running it on a laptop in LM Studio and that it drew a better pelican than Opus 4.7; others praised the Qwen team for still shipping open weights.

items.name: LM Studio
items.source_story_title: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B: Agentic coding power, now open to all
items.summary: Local AI desktop app for running models privately on your own machine.
items.why_included: It surfaced as the practical way commenters were actually running the new Qwen model locally.
items.evidence: A Qwen commenter said they were running the model on their laptop in LM Studio.

items.name: Unsloth
items.source_story_title: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B: Agentic coding power, now open to all
items.summary: Open-source tooling for quantizing and running models locally.
items.why_included: It was singled out as the tool that made the new Qwen release easy to use locally.
items.evidence: A commenter noted that Qwen was already quantized and converted into a sane format by Unsloth.

items.name: Android CLI
items.source_story_title: Android CLI: Build Android apps 3x faster using any agent
items.summary: Google's CLI for building Android apps faster with agents.
items.why_included: Commenters immediately mapped it to real workflows and asked for the same thing on Apple.
items.evidence: People asked how to use it with Claude Code, praised the promise of proper requirements and flexible tooling, and one wished Apple had an equivalent.

items.name: Kampala
items.source_story_title: Launch HN: Kampala (YC W26) – Reverse-Engineer Apps into APIs
items.summary: Tool for reverse-engineering web apps into APIs and OpenAPI specs.
items.why_included: The comments showed concrete agent workflows around SPA scraping, HAR analysis, and API extraction.
items.evidence: Commenters described using it to connect Claude Code to third-party web apps and to analyze HAR files into OpenAPI specs; others asked about SSL pinning.

items.name: Artifacts
items.source_story_title: Artifacts: Versioned storage that speaks Git
items.summary: Cloudflare's Git-flavored versioned storage for agent artifacts.
items.why_included: The discussion focused on real tradeoffs like backend compatibility, batching, and storage costs.
items.evidence: Commenters liked the ArtifactFS demos, wanted an S3-compatible backend, and noted the PUT/POST cost difference versus S3.

items.name: Claude Code
items.source_story_title: Show HN: CodeBurn – Analyze Claude Code token usage by task
items.summary: Anthropic's agentic coding system for terminal and web workflows.
items.why_included: It kept surfacing as the coding agent people are actually using in the wild.
items.evidence: A CodeBurn commenter said they have Claude write plans before every coding session, and another said they had a fun Claude Code experience driving a Bluetooth device.

items.name: CodeBurn
items.source_story_title: Show HN: CodeBurn – Analyze Claude Code token usage by task
items.summary: Small tool for analyzing Claude Code token usage by task.
items.why_included: People immediately saw the value in visibility and started discussing next-step analytics.
items.evidence: Commenters called it cool, liked the terminal UI approach, and suggested adding inefficiency detection.

items.name: Tree-sitter
items.source_story_title: A Better R Programming Experience Thanks to Tree-sitter
items.summary: Incremental parsing library that powers richer editor intelligence across languages.
items.why_included: The thread drew explicit praise for the project's broad utility and engineering quality.
items.evidence: A commenter called Tree-sitter one of the finer engineering products out there and said it enables so much.

items.name: MacMind
items.source_story_title: Show HN: MacMind – A transformer neural network in HyperCard on a 1989 Macintosh
items.summary: HyperCard demo of a transformer neural net running on a 1989 Macintosh.
items.why_included: Retro AI-on-old-hardware is exactly the kind of delightful constraint play HN latches onto.
items.evidence: Commenters called it quietly impressive, loved the old-HyperCard feel, and said it reminded them of early backprop days.

items.name: Playdate
items.source_story_title: Playdate’s handheld changed how Duke University teaches game design
items.summary: Panic's quirky handheld game console, now being used in a game-design classroom.
items.why_included: The discussion mixed hands-on praise with real product tradeoffs and classroom use.
items.evidence: A commenter said the developer experience has been fantastic, while others debated the lack of backlight and the price.
